channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Liverpool's lead cut in the Premier League and Man City finally ends winless run
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Liverpool's lead at the top of the Premier League was cut to seven points after a thrilling 3-3 draw with Newcastle on Wednesday. Chelsea moved up to second by thrashing last-place Southampton 5-1, while Arsenal is third after a 2-0 win over Manchester United.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trevor Lawrence placed on injured reserve, likely ending his season after dangerous hit
item.description: The Jacksonville Jaguars have placed Trevor Lawrence on injured reserve, likely ending his season after the hit he took earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lindsey Vonn to make her comeback to downhill racing at age 40 this weekend
item.description: At age 40, Lindsey Vonn is going to enter an official ski race for the first time in nearly six years this weekend.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: SEC and Big Ten powers lead the way on signing day as prospects finalize their college selections
item.description: Georgia, Texas, Alabama, Oregon and Ohio State are bringing in the nation's top recruiting class as high school prospects across the country finalize their college plans.
item.dc:creator: Steve Megargee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: LPGA and USGA to require players to be assigned female at birth or transition before male puberty
item.description: The LPGA and the USGA have updated gender policies that will require players to be assigned female at birth or to have transitioned to female before going through male puberty.
item.dc:creator: Doug Ferguson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bay Area News Group high school football predictions: Week 15, 2024
item.description: The guys pick eight NorCal regional championship games involving Bay Area teams, including Pittsburg-Folsom, Amador Valley-McClymonds, Leland-Moreau Catholic
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Jaguars sign Super Bowl champion quarterback after Trevor Lawrence injury
item.description: The Jacksonville Jaguars bolstered its quarterback room on Tuesday with the signing of John Wolford, the former Los Angeles Rams QB won a Super Bowl with them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court can take massive step in preventing trans athletes in girls' sports with historic hearing
item.description: The Supreme Court faces a decision that could help determine whether biological males will compete against female athletes for generations to come.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jazz commit 29 turnovers in 133-106 loss to OKC
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz lead the NBA in turnovers, and Tuesday night was their worst night yet. Utah committed a season-high 29 turnovers, 19 in the first half, as the Jazz go 0-4 in NBA Cup games with a 133-106 blowout loss at Oklahoma City.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jill Ellis steps down from club president position with San Diego Wave FC
item.description: Jill Ellis, club president of the San Diego Wave FC, will be stepping down from her role with the team, the club announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Knicks beat Magic to advance in the NBA Cup. Orlando also moves on as the East wild card
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Karl-Anthony Towns had 23 points and 15 rebounds, Jalen Brunson scored 21 points and the New York Knicks beat the Orlando Magic 121-106 on Tuesday to reach the quarterfinals of the NBA Cup. New York went 4-0 in East Group A and will host Atlanta on Dec. 11.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan fires offensive coordinator Kirk Campbell despite team's upset win over Ohio State
item.description: Michigan head football coach Sherrone Moore announced the firing of offensive coordinator Kirk Campbell Tuesday despite the team's upset of Ohio State.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: St. John's to honor late coach Lou Carnesecca at men's and women's games this weekend
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The men's and women's basketball teams at St. John's will wear “Lou” patches on their uniform jerseys for the rest of this season to honor Hall of Fame coach Lou Carnesecca, who died Saturday at age 99. A funeral mass at St.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nick Rimando elected to National Soccer Hall of Fame
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Former Real Salt Lake goalkeeper Nick Rimando has been elected to the National Soccer Hall of Fame. Rimando will be enshrined with the Class of 2025 on May 3, 2025 in Frisco, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Lou Carnesecca, New York basketball legend, passes away at 99
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Lou Carnesecca is a New York legend. Carnesecca passed away over the weekend at the age of 99. He lived a wonderful life, and his impact was unquestioned on and off the basketball court. Born on Jan. 5, 1925, he was the son of Italian immigrants. He attended St.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: 2024 PPR All 51 Team
item.description: 2024 PPR All 51 Team: Each season our selection committee recognizes the best players from across the county, assembling our "All 51" team. This list includes our first team picks, and honorable mentions.
item.dc:creator: Maddy Worley, Fox 5

item.title: McCaffrey injury potentially rules him out for rest of season
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — San Francisco running back Christian McCaffrey suffered a knee injury that coach Kyle Shanahan described as “potentially” season-ending in the 49ers’ 35-10 loss to the Buffalo Bills on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Ryan Poles to remain Bears general manager and lead search for new head coach
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. (AP) — While his first head coaching hire is now out, Ryan Poles is still the general manager of the Chicago Bears — for now.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago Sky, Angel Reese to open 2025 season against Indiana Fever, Caitlin Clark
item.description: Angel Reese and Caitlin Clark won't have to wait long to renew their rivalry during the next WNBA season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Mitchell scores 35, Cavs end Celtics' winning streak with 115-111 win in matchup of East's elite
item.description: Donovan Mitchell scored 20 of his 35 points in the fourth quarter as the Cleveland Cavaliers ended Boston's seven-game winning streak with a 115-111 victory over the Celtics on Sunday night in the second intense matchup between the Eastern Conference’s top teams this season.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Monahan with goal and assist, Blue Jackets extend points streak to 6 with 6-3 win over Blackhawks
item.description: Sean Monahan had a goal and assist, and the Columbus Blue Jackets extended their points streak to six games with a 6-3 win over the Chicago Blackhawks on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ohio State, Michigan players involved in melee after Buckeyes loss, multiple people pepper sprayed
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — Michigan players planted their team flag at midfield of Ohio Stadium on Saturday afternoon after a 13-10 win over No. 2 Ohio State, leading to a brawl between players from both teams with police attempting to separate the sides and pepper spray being used.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, WGN 9

item.title: Bears fire coach Matt Eberflus after skid marked by poor decisions late in games
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Matt Eberflus was fired by the Chicago Bears on Friday, one day after botching a timeout in a loss at Detroit. Offensive coordinator Thomas Brown will serve as interim coach, the Bears said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN and ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers extend contract of postseason hero
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers have agreed to a contract extension with one of the key pieces of the team's World Series title run. The Dodgers reached agreement Friday on a deal with Tommy Edman, ensuring that the do-it-all utility-man will remain with the team for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: 49ers to likely play Bills in heavy snow, 'dangerously cold wind chills': NWS
item.description: (KRON) -- In last week's 38-10 blowout loss to the Green Bay Packers, the 49ers' loss had little to do with the weather at Lambeau Field, also known as "The Frozen Tundra." At kickoff, it was a calm 42 degrees and mostly cloudy in the typical chilly Midwest city.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Depleted Jazz lose to Denver, 122-103
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Without injured stars Lauri Markkanen, John Collins and Jordan Clarkson, the Utah Jazz had little chance against the Denver Nuggets and lost for the sixth time in their last seven games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
